ClientIntake.CompanyName: Adam & Karmel LLC dba A&K Laundry Basket

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
I met with Karmel and Elizabeth Propp of CMURC on Friday, 4/25, to discuss the Great Lakes Bay Small Business Hub grant. Karmel filled Elizabeth in on the business, and how she plans on using the funds to support the community with her token program. It was a very good emotional appeal, and Elizabeth walked her through the dashboard to access the application documents.

Karmel asked some questions about acceptable use of funds, and we discussed other items that she may like to purchase, like smaller washers for her elderly customers. I also reinforced that David and I are here to help review documents as needed.

Our client contacted me by email sharing a letter from her attorney regarding the current administration's efforts in tightening the use of FHA and SBA loan programs by foreign nationals and non-permanent alien residents in the US. The stricter guidelines appear to take away the option of SBA financing for my client. Email: https://michigansbdcatgrandvalleystateuniv.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/EmailMessage/02sVs00000QA5lpIAD/view


More feedback from Paul Scoby: Isabella Bank is at its limit for restaurant and hospitality loans. They have done quite a bit of rewriting loans in the last 2-4 years and have reached their concentration limit in those areas.

Discussed market research. With this project, the free data is hard to come by. I discussed using BAF for market study report in cases like this. I sent the following to the client:


We’re going to forecast three things:

 

  • Drone motors
  • Other non-auto/truck emobility vehicles (bikes, scooters, golf carts, etc.)
  • 300+Kw drive motors

 

Establish a unit along with growth rates. We can limit it to US only. I don’t care about the distinction between addressable and beachhead markets, since I use an expected long term market capture rate to drive the actual forecast of units sold by Modal Motors.

Media.Other: SBDC Client - Internet Search for business assistance

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Phone call with Joe, he feels like he has reached his capacity to grow without help. He wants to hire additional sales people but he is the best person at sales.



RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

I asked him to send me a P&L report and suggested he make a list of things that he does that he would like to get off his plate. We will review those and then determine which direction to move forward in hiring.


NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS:

he is going to work on that and get back to me





IMPACT REMINDER: client is aware of need for attribution

After reviewing the client's responses and looking at the overall operations of the business operating everything manually I would not think that this is a good business to purchase. After speaking with Christie she's deciding to pass on this particular opportunity, but still has several questions on what she needs to consider when purchasing a business.


So far she's been looking for opportunities Biz Buy Sell and talking to small business brokers who she feels aren't reputable because they haven't been honest as it relates to the businesses they are representing.


Initially the client will not be working full time in the business and is looking for an opportunity that has a couple of employees who can operate the business in her absence and until she learns the operations of the business. She's looked a business that have sales prices as high as 1 million. (Christie has $200,000 to invest.)


I have advised he

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Visit to Client Site to see open distillery

Our client is looking for sales training for a single employee at his business. He was interested in what the SBDC had to offer and I could not find anything directly related to sales force training among what we offer. I contacted Autumn Matthews who included Tim Booms in the conversation who came up with a 3rd party online course solution called "Sales Training for Individuals."


I sent the course outline information and its cost, $725, to our client to see if this fits what he is looking for regarding his employee. He is to contact Tim directly if he wants to pursue the training. Email: https://michigansbdcatgrandvalleystateuniv.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/EmailMessage/02sVs00000Q9W6gIAF/view

I spoke with Mr. Koyluoglu about his business. Mr. Koyluogy explained to me that he is a classically trained Chef with over 10 years experience in Culinary Cuisine. He also share with me that his culinary experience include working in several upscale restaurants such as Selden Standard and Madam. Mr. Koyluogy long term goal is to open his own sit down upscale restaurant however, he feels that his best approach to reach that goal is to start by marketing his culinary skills via pop up shops. Mr. Koyluogy also explained that he has conducted several pop up shops in his target area and had attendance over hundred people. I inquired about the types of food focus he plan to offer and he indicated that he will be focusing on Turkish cuisine. I then ask have he indentified any competition and he shared with me he had identified two restaurants that are sit down in Dearborn and in Sterling Heights. The great thing about our conversation is that Mr. Koyluogylu realizes the difficulties an

Phone check in with Bob to discuss progress in LivePlan. He is hoping to finish the forecast within 1 week and will notify me when it is ready for review. I recommended that he keep his projections sufficient to demonstrate the ability to pay back debt and cover living expenses, but not make them too rosy, which would make the bank nervous. Next step: review business plan when Bob is ready. He is currently talking to SNBT about financing.

Adrienne and Nancy discussed the challenges of developing an MVP for their product, particularly in the EdTech sector, and explored the potential of using AI to generate educational content. They also discussed the need for a clear and concise value proposition to present to Doug, who is interested in equity in the community, and the importance of enhancing the current lesson structure to make it more engaging and personalized for students. Lastly, they discussed the process of creating interactive elements in a click-through presentation and the challenges of working with such presentations.


Next steps

Adrienne to create a basic lesson structure using Gagne's steps.

Adrienne to find an "ugly" lesson example from current educational tools like IXL.

Adrienne to redesign the "ugly" lesson example using Color Genius approach for two personas (e.g., one inspired by Simone Biles, one by Kobe

JD is working through several challenges as he refines his company's pitch, prepares for funding, and plans for national expansion. He's balancing the need to revise financial projections to satisfy stakeholders with maintaining realistic expectations for growth. JD is facing hurdles in increasing gym onboarding rates, managing customer acquisition costs, and adjusting marketing budgets. He's also navigating how to best tell the customer story in his pitch, optimize ARR projections, and better align his financial plan with fundraising milestones. With rapid scaling on the horizon, JD needs to tighten up cash flow planning and strategically time market entries into Columbus, Cleveland, and Cincinnati.

I recommended that JD update his pitch deck and narrative to center the story around employees of small businesses as the primary customer, refine his traction slide to focus only on the most impressive recent metrics, and structure his deck around a clear solution/pro

Went over BAF submission process, sizing, sequencing and criteria. Discussed international rollout with opportunities in UK, Japan and Poland to date. Advisable to forego flexibility and lock down IP in expectation of substantial near term opportunities world wide. Swept has secured renewed investor interest after closing on M25 funding and may run into BAF cap, and also be oversubscribed.

Third attempt

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Quick recap 

Nancy and Janet discussed the weather and the software customer discovery and validation questions, with Nancy offering to share a worksheet containing this information. They also discussed the importance of using tried and true methods in their work, the possibility of submitting additional software upgrades for a client's product, and the challenges of working with a founder who is struggling to understand the concept of customer discovery. The conversation also touched on the progress of a project at the ballpark and the potential involvement of Shelby Kelsch and Adam Bazaar.


Next steps

Janet to introduce Nancy to Adam Bazaar from Cradle Me.

Janet to follow up with Shelby Kelsch regarding potential software prototyping opportunities.

Janet to present Tracy's software upgrade proposal to Toni for BAF consideration.

Nancy to meet with Shelby Kelsch as scheduled to discuss softw

Phone call with Roger to talk about expanding his frozen pizza line. He is having trouble with getting licensing through mdard due to packaging concerns. I referred him to the MSU extension product center. I also let him know that his llc annual statements have not been received since 2018 and he needs to contact Michigan lara to get his llc reinstated. I sent him emails on how to reinstate his llc and offered to help him as needed .

Reviewed Letter of Support from CEDIA, and strategized leveraging it for the next association, and provided a fully executed comprehensive example from a past successful business as a target to shoot for.

Our client was able to call me back today. She has been very busy with her new practice and seems to be keeping up on everything. She noted that her revenues are $38K to date this year and she averages $900/month in expenses of which rent is $500 (she rents office space from a chiropractor) and about $400 for electronic medical reporting and credit card processing. She had to establish a waiting list as she is currently at her capacity of 5 to 6 patients per day for hour long sessions plus another hour and a half for administrative work. She refers students in need to the University Counseling center and others to Community Mental Health when the need appears more urgent. If she were to add another clinician she would need more space and there isn't any more available where she is at. It would also change the dynamic of her practice and her responsibilities. She said that the demand is great right now and she hasn't been able to refer to other offices than previously

04/25/2025




90 Day Business Refinement Cohort Information

Hi John!


Great talking with you today!


As promised, attached is an application and overview of the 90 Day Business Cohort I'm planning to get going at the beginning of May. Please forward it along to anyone who might benefit from the class!


The purpose of the program is to help legitimize side-hustle operations and prepare small business owners to expand activity beyond the borders of their friends and family circle. Participants meet online weekly to enhance their business knowledge, connect with fellow entrepreneurs, and gain valuable insights from guest speak

Nikki is working through several challenges as she builds SideDoor, a platform to help first-time homebuyers navigate the process more independently and affordably. She's balancing the need to clearly define the platform’s market positioning, create a compelling pitch deck, and refine financial forecasts to support upcoming investor conversations. She's also figuring out how to best show traction, structure her target market, and adapt the business model around title companies and mortgage lender partnerships. Additionally, she’s facing decisions around marketing strategies to attract more buyers and transitioning the business from transaction-based to scalable revenue streams.

I recommended that Nikki restructure her pitch deck around a tighter, more investor-focused flow: Solution, Problem, Traction, Team, Financial Forecast, and Exit Strategy. We agreed she should create a traction slide that focuses only on the most impressive and recent metrics and add real acquis

Scott Hotz is working through several challenges as he advances his battery technology startup. He’s refining his pitch and financial model to prepare for an upcoming presentation to Michigan’s Innovation Partnerships team and future investors. He’s also navigating company formation, funding strategies (including balancing non-dilutive grants and private investment), and planning for early sales pilots. Additionally, he's facing hurdles in hiring a capable VP of Sales who can work independently, structuring a go-to-market plan, properly accounting for financial details like asset purchases, and managing presentation confidentiality without overcomplicating technical explanations.


I recommended that Scott refine the pitch deck to focus more on building excitement than technical education, using a clear structure: a strong problem/solution story in the first 3 minutes, followed by business and growth strategy in the next 7. I advised updating the financial model t

JD is working through several challenges as he refines his company's pitch, prepares for funding, and plans for national expansion. He's balancing the need to revise financial projections to satisfy stakeholders with maintaining realistic expectations for growth. JD is facing hurdles in increasing gym onboarding rates, managing customer acquisition costs, and adjusting marketing budgets. He's also navigating how to best tell the customer story in his pitch, optimize ARR projections, and better align his financial plan with fundraising milestones. With rapid scaling on the horizon, JD needs to tighten up cash flow planning and strategically time market entries into Columbus, Cleveland, and Cincinnati.

I recommended that JD update his pitch deck and narrative to center the story around employees of small businesses as the primary customer, refine his traction slide to focus only on the most impressive recent metrics, and structure his deck around a clear solution/pro
Responded to client's pitchbook request. Reviewing pitchbook reports.

Met with Keymarie Thompson who has natural body creme and hair oil products, which she is currently making in her kitchen. She is currently enrolled in the ProsperUs entrepreneurial training program, that I referred her to and is now identifying contract manufacturers and how to gain distribution in hotels and health food stores. We have discussed the process of contracting with a manufacturer, providing them with the formula, getting samples, packaging and shipping. We will meet again in two weeks to determine progress.

Jesse, after our discussion yesterday, I had a couple of additional thoughts.

Hi Tom,


Thank you for your message. Yes, I remember you and we are here to help! Please be introduced to Kevin Manninen (copied), who can guide you through the process.

I Spoke with Ms. Lee Mattes about her business idea. Ms. Mattes explained to me that she has been baking Sour Dough Bread for many years and has been selling her bread at farmer's markets. Ms. Mattes indicated that she believes that it is a market for her product and she would like to be able to mass produce her bread products. Ms. Mattes reach out to us because she is seeking financial assistance and is hoping we can help her in realizing her business goals. Ms. Mattes explained one of the benefits of her bread is that it is easy digestible and perfect for people that have difficulties digesting bread. Her long term goal strategy is to buy a property and convert it into a kitchen that would allow her to mass produce her product and to able to grow her on crops to manufacture wholesome baked goods. Currently, Ms. Mattes is in a boot camp program and she is in the third week of a six week program to assist her in developing her business plan. I suggested some additional products

Why it matters for quarterly taxes:

The IRS cares about net income (income minus expenses) earned in the current year, not lifetime totals.

If your sheet shows the net income for the year, and it's over a certain threshold (roughly $1,000 or more in net income), then you'll probably need to start making estimated quarterly payments.

ClientIntake.BusinessType: Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Case Closed

I spoke with Susan regarding her housing project she had no progress on the business plan and cash flow. I reiterated that all financing starts with the business plan and cash flow.


We got into the discussion of what is expected of the her from the bank and she was surprised by the information that the banks will require 10-20% down on all lending. She was upset and I simply explained to her that it proves the bank that you have stake in the project and the full faith that you will be able to pay back the loan. Susan explained to me that she will be unable to secure any funding if banks require any sort of down payment

It is going a bit slow. The building search continues. Rob has been great. We also had another supporter offer some financial support - so many people in the pool community want this to happen and give us their support. So, more to come soon - hopefully. Thanks for checking in.

I met with Travis, who shared his idea of becoming a sales agent for a company that markets and builds easy-to-construct garages and buildings. He envisions this as a side job while he continues to work in IT at Tech.


Travis's idea was inspired by his personal experience purchasing a building from the company. He believes he could provide better service to clients than the current sales representative. During our conversation, he wanted to discuss options and gather feedback on his plan. Specifically, he was considering whether to operate under a "Doing Business As" (DBA) name or to set up a Limited Liability Company (LLC). I told him that the decision could come later.


I provided him with a simple business plan and encouraged him to write down his thoughts and develop a more concrete plan. He plans to gather more information and will get back to me at a later date.

Again, it might change. Sent: Monday, April 28, 2025 2:28 PM

To: Karlie Robinson <

Absolutely! I attached two financial support spreadsheets to this email.


The "Monthly Cash Flow" spreadsheet monitors expenses, revenue and will auto calculate the profit from month to month. I added some examples in blue text so you can see how this may look. For each month, you'll create a new sheet within that document.



The "Price Point Determination" helps figure out what price to make your product/service. There are three separate columns for three separate products/services. You may have more or less than three, just adjust the sheet accordingly. Add in values in the blue boxes only, and the rest will auto calculate.


Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns, I would be happy to advise!

I spoke with Sarah regarding grant application materials this afternoon. She had a few questions regarding the executive summary, as well as her funding ask:

  • Sarah was reading through the Exec Summary template, and asked about length. I recommended that she answer all questions listed for a 5-page executive summary for submission.
  • She also had questions about building her financial projections. I suggested that she use historical data as a starting point. Sarah did some research and found that 6-7% growth is predicted in the industry over the next 5 years. This is all information that we can use to build her projections. I did advised that while accuracy is best, the reviewers are looking for reasonable projections rather than exact predictions.
  • We discussed other areas that reviewers prioritized in the past. I recommended adding information on mentors and advisors that the team utilize - SBDC and colleagues in the industry are great.
  • Sarah also m

I met with Deanna at the shop to begin working on the updated grant proposal, as well as get some updates on the business. Deanna said that business is going very well - they are currently performing a trial run with Kindel furniture maker of Grand Rapids. Kindel makes high-quality furniture, ranging from $200 per dining room chair to up to $14,000 for a sofa. Kindel provided Mid MI Awning & Upholstery with several chairs and two couches to upholster. They will then be inspected, and upon approval, they will be upholstering Kindel's overflow.

After a tour of the shop floor, we talked grants. Deanna wants to change their ask to focus more on project management - preferably purchasing new computers to track progress, as well as new work stations for the administrators. We took a look at the scores from last round, and the first two reviews were consistent - mid to high 80s. However, their final score was 37. Deanna fears that the third reviewer may have been from Gratio

Call with Karyn. She is still trying to negotiate with the family that owns the house in Lake City. They asked for $3300 in rent. They also want to bring Bill, the adult in need of care who owns the home, home. That could complicate the process as she tries to get licensed. They also offered her some kind of tiered rent for the house. But they don't know that she will be full the first month. I encouraged her to stop sharing information about her business operations with the landlord family, like what she is charging people or other data. They also probably want her to rent the house since its a lot money, more than they'll get on the market in LC. She has some leverage. She isn't sure she wants to pay the fee to start licensing because she doesn't know if it will work out with this family. It's only $200. She can afford it. So, not much action, but she did say she'd share any documents with me before she signs anything. Speaking again in a week.

Great Lakes Finance

Kalamazoo Forward Ventures

Hi Kate,

I was asking my wife the questions you sent me in the last email. The $2,012.50/week is what she is making now and it's before the expenses are taken out. Her cost for an assistant is only $250-$300/month. The cost for an assistant is very low because my wife will schedule her part-time and full-time kids on my days off and she can be over 6. Then on the days I work, she will make sure to only have 6 kids during the day. Then when the 6 school-aged kids get off the bus, either my mom comes over or the neighbor lady comes. The neighbor lady gets paid $25 everyday she comes. She is only here 1-2 hours at the most. When school gets out, she will probably have 12 kids each day. The weekly income will go up but so will the assistant amount. What numbers should we use?  

She currently has 16 kids enrolled. That's a combination of part-time and full-time. July 27, she will be able to have 14 kids on any given day as long as she has an assistant working.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Reviewed slide deck and practiced Momentum Pitch. Troy did a great job creating his slide deck and his pitch was 5 minutes 10 seconds. We discussed if he wants to have time for his closing comments, to cut some of the financial and let the slide speak for itself. I asked Troy some questions like, what is your background? What in your background will make you successful with being a small business owner? Talk about your employees you will have, when, how you will recruit? Troy is hoping they will ask him if he has anything in addition he would like them to know. I stated, if he has not used all of the Q&A time, he could close by adding a few more details he was not able to include within the 5 minutes. All of my questions he had a solid response to.


Plan: We will practice one more time with him advancing the slides, to see if this cuts down on the 5 minutes. This will be on Tuesday, May 6th at 12:30pm.

Met with Louise today to continue working on her strategy. Louise continues to be focused on making her business strong. She has signed two break out sessions , a podcast appearance and an ethics training workshop. She also updated her marketing OKR's to reflect measurable outcomes, timings and associated tasks. Recommendations: Louise is focused on making sure her business is sustainable and we have been in discussion about how she can pivot to corporate buyers with the services she offers. CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Virtual CMURC Grant Intake / Manual Prep of Consent Form / Email Follow-up


Intake Description:

Brent Gettel was on the intake call for the Direct to Business Grant program. The program is administered by CMURC and Elisabeth Propp discussed program details. SBDC consultant discussed our role in the program which is to assist them with any questions encountered while preparing the Executive Summary application. The application is effectively a business plan which is required for submitting a completed application to the thinkific portal.  CMURC described the portal used for the application process and the Right Choice Program which provides training based on various steppingstones of business operation. Follow-up emails sent for contact information with CMURC and SBDC.

Along with Emily Gibson, met with Sarah at Morey Tech Center.

  • Sarah updated us on her departure last year from Family Home Health Care, and how the deal she was promised with the two brother owners fell through. The main owner she had been working with has developed dementia and doesn't remember some of the details, and other brother didn't want to proceed with sale. There was no formal agreement in place so she has no recourse.
  • Because of this, she is now able to focus on Love 2 Care. She is slowly growing her client base and her employee base. While these numbers are somewhat fluid, she currently has 22 patients. She also has 22 employee (counting herself), 3 of which are full-time and the remaining 19 are part-time however many of them work 30-40 hours a week.
  • She is averaging between $20k and $30k a month in revenue. Despite having mostly 3rd party pay (Medicaid), she is not having cash flow issues, and is even able to pay herself. She was advi

Along with Jeff Punches, I met with Sarah at the Morey Tech Center to introduce myself as her new primary consultant.

Sarah filled us in on how the business is going - she has a good balance of clients and employees - around 22 of each. She also told us about her experience parting ways with Family Home & Health Care, and how that purchase will not be moving forward.

For now, she is planning on focusing her efforts on better marketing herself to employees. She has a good flow of clients, and often gets referrals from families and friends. Moving forward, she is going to focus on getting her audience to know her as the face of the business. We will meet in early June to discuss strategy and complete a social audit as needed.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Researched and shared several resources with client per request in our meeting


TRose Malone <trose.businesscoach@gmail.com<

Quick recap 

Kevin and Nancy discussed strategies for targeting new customers and promoting Vouch, focusing on specific verticals and utilizing data from Shopify. They explored the potential of competing with Amazon by attracting customers loyal to influencers and discussed the growth and scaling of Kevin's platform, including incentivizing influencers and measuring success through metrics. The conversation concluded with next steps for Kevin's business, including engaging with an accelerator, applying for a grant, and addressing challenges faced by small businesses in rural areas.

Next steps

Matt wanted to discuss a business assembling wire harnesses for equipment manufacturers. We discussed whether there is a real market for these. His idea comes from his own experience working for an area equipment manufacturer.

Right now his idea is to start small, perhaps in his own garage.

I advised him to try to talk to a few potential customers to see if there is a need and if the idea is even feasible.

Showed him the Simple Business Plan and suggested that he start listing the financial requirements of starting such a business.

He will set up another appointment once he has done some research.

Hi Nicole, 


The free legal help is from Michiganlegalhelp.org. Depending on what you need help with, you may need to get an attorney or Legal Zoom. 

Regarding the Registered Agent, you are only buying a mailing address to use for LARA (State of Michigan) correspondence. It cannot be a PO Box and must be a physical address that is "maned". The other option could be a Mailbox at a UPS store or some storage units. The general cost is $50 per year for registered agents and there is a popular choice that is in Okemos https://www.michiganregisteredagent.com/. 

I personally think it isn't necessary and you should just use your home address. Clients can Google you to get your home address if they want to find you. Insurance and other mailings you would pr

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

The owner has signed a lease for a new retail space and is transitioning to full-time entrepreneurship, no longer working outside the business. The store is set to open the second week of June, with the buildout approximately halfway complete. The lease includes three months of free rent to accommodate buildout and launch preparation. The business will offer boutique and boudoir items, including wood signs and handmade cards, and plans to generate revenue through local referral partnerships with nail salons, hair salons, and spas.


With the owner now financially dependent on the business, there is pressure to generate revenue quickly. Marketing and digital visibility efforts are in progress but incomplete. While some physical branding components (like signage) are pending permit approval, other key visibility tools—like the website and ad campaigns—require immedia
sent email to check in
Sent email to touch base

Reviewed client business plan and detailed financials as client preps her loan application. Provided input and feedback on specific ideas to add to her plan. Client intimated that they had a new capital instance as they prep for their loan.

Farrand Hall and Farrand Hall Bakery and Farrand Hall Market are located in Colon, Michigan and seeking funding to expand and do more economic development in the Colon community and possibly the surrounding farm area. We have been working on revising the business plan and investment and grant opportunities. We have had virtual meetings with Jennifer from MDARD and Sarah from MEDC. It was decided that MDARD was the appropriate source and Jennifer invited and suggested that we attend the grant webinar in preparation of applying for funding on the June 11, proposal deadline cycle. We did and discussed how the funding guideline could benefit FH and how to proceed. James and Jennifer are communicating and he has decided to pursue the funding for equipment and salary reimbursement. He is gathering the data and starting the grant. He will reach out if there is support from SBDC and if funded the impact will be collected.
Weekly mentoring session
check in email

Zoom with Sridhar to update on recent events, fundraising efforts and next steps. He and David had productive sessions with Accelerate Blue, ID Ventures, Red Cedar, Spark Capital and City Side Ventures. Data room access granted and due diligence is proceeding. Follow up session with City Side is pending. Also presented at UnDemo Day. Session with Assembly Ventures is coming up . Though significant interest, none are willing to lead a round. Sridhar reported disappointment and perhaps misunderstanding with David not will to kick in investment dollars. Also reported that they did not win two Phase II DoD SBIR awards, which is very disappointing. Feedback from program manager was that there wasn't enough war-fighter solutions content, didn't see the tech and plan as sufficient to hit Phase III commercialization goals, and that there were differing priorities amongst the DoD reviewers. ImpliFi addressed some of the problems, but there were others on the table -- like 60km

Summary of our meeting with the client today:


ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM


The client is preparing for the birth of her first child in about four weeks and is focused on organizing her business operations to allow for a smoother transition into maternity leave. While she has made strong progress in launching her holistic wellness certification program, including strategic use of initial Cohort funding, she now needs to maintain business momentum during her upcoming downtime. She also requires support with bookkeeping, tax planning, and expanding her program reach through partnerships and community engagement.


SUMMARY OF BUSINESS IDEA AND TOPICS DISCUSSED

Sankofa Aya: consulting business but wants to create a “hybrid art, coffee, cocktail space”

Steps to Register a Foreign LLC in Michigan:

  1. Check Name Availability – Your LLC’s name must be unique and not conflict with existing Michigan business names.
  2. File an Application for Certificate of Authority – Submit Form 760 (Application for Certificate of Authority to Transact Business in Michigan).
  3. Appoint a Registered Agent in Michigan – You must have a registered agent with a physical address in Michigan.
  4. Obtain a Certificate of Good Standing – You need this from your home state to show your LLC is in good standing. (this you get from the current state)
  5. Pay the Filing Fee – Michigan's filing fee for a foreign LLC registra

Total jobs, including yourself and contractual employees

I have reviewed the business plan and have the following recommendations: 


In our previous meeting you indicated that you were buying an existing business. The business plan is very vague to the details, such as the "business is located in Warren". There is only a sales forecast. Have you received the tax returns? They would be a requirement for the bank. 


What are the sources and uses for funds? 


The marketing section includes numerous possible marketing tools including billboards, but it is not listed as an expense. In the business plan, you should only have what you are actually going to do in month one. 


Wendy Richardson | Business Consultant

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Summary: The client wrote thanking me for the instructions to maximize engagement with the programs and their managers. This past week, he has met with other researchers at various Universities who have shared their interest toward collaborative efforts to assess the efficacy of CAPNOS devices in trials. He is awaiting scoring feedback on the R21 that has been submitted to see if there is an opportunity for funding, which could help inform how the outreach efforts may proceed regarding SBIR/STTR funding. It is his goal to spend much of May as a liaison between researchers, grant writers, communicating with program managers, and other interested parties to work with health insurance payers or quit programs.


Action: I responded that it was good to hear from them and thanked them for this update.  I *finally* had a chance to look at the link that they sent in an earlier email for the NIMHD STTR that they talked about briefly on the phone. It is (mostly) a standard STT

Email/response


It's not that I can't get them. I should be able to get them easily from the owner. The owner is working with a business broker to sell the business. I have to talk to the broker about getting the tax documents.


On Wed, Apr 30, 2025, 2:12 PM Wendy Richardson <wrichar2@emich.edu> wrote:

Without the tax returns, it is very hard to continue. If the owner will not release, that may indicate a problem with the revenue. At the end of the day, the bank will require them. If you do not know what is in them, it could be a nasty surprise. 


Also you should be working with the owner on how the business is being run today. You can also have what you are changing. Currently, the business plan reads like a startup. 

Wendy Richardson | Business Consultant

Next steps:

  1. Shawn to report back on findings.

Client reported that she will be meeting with Loretta this evening, the lady selling the liquor license on getting an extension. She will update me. She is also in the process of applying for her E2 visa which would allow her to be here for five years to operate a business.

Comms with client:


portfolio and background for reviewExternal

Inbox

Jim Eisenbeis

Attended Amy's open house. Gorgeous place. Nice Bed & Breakfast. Still not as busy as she would like. Discussed how they would rent out the whole place. Discussed future event venue, parade float for PR.

Jim has an interest in developing a series of products using wax cotton. He has extensive background in the apparel industry, most recently working with B May bags in Harbour Springs. He describes the product he is developing as apparel accessories. His desire is to have control over his his designs.


He is looking for market research. More specifically he is looking for contract manufacturers for his product designs.


We talked extensively about the demand side and identifying market potential for his designs.


additionally, we discussed the local market for retail of outdoor recreation goods. He is very interested in having products available locally as well as manufactured domestically and more specifically in Michigan if possible.


Consultant to provide connections to wholesale distributors in the outdoor recreation, apparel industry, which are located in Harbor Springs area.


As for cadence, he felt

As promised, I provided a template for a business plan and created a spreadsheet for Heather's childcare facility and will be ready to address questions and edit her plan as it develops.


email to Heather:

Heather, I enjoyed our conversation yesterday ad am confident that I can help you develop the business plan you need for your loan application.

 

I have attached two documents. The first is my template for a business plan and it will show what we need to include in your plan.

 

The second document is a cash flow spreadsheet. You can populate the bottom section of the sheet to develop revenue streams and payroll numbers. The formulas are already in place.

The interview actually went well, but come to find out, they only wanted an RD who was exclusive to them and who didn’t already have a private practice. I didn’t want to give up what I currently have as it’s been steady with Berrystreet. So, staying put for a bit :)

Thank you for the follow-up. 

Rosina

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
**CONSULTATION**

After having met with client Miguel to review his written business plan and financials a session ago, he has since completed his LLC and EIN, with only the Michigan Sales Tax License remaining before we move into 1- and 3-year projections and specific food trailer requirements (with support from Wendy or Siera). I shared information about the 20+ hour consulting reimbursement opportunity and El Concilio’s resources, both of which he was interested in. I also noted his potential impact as a new SBDC business and plan to follow up on that soon.

• Long lead time for new proposals
  • Good that he has all the up-front stuff
  • $800 income in Q1 (Only really 2 months of work yay!)
  • Got business insurance
  • Questions
    • Health insurance is good until September,but what should he do after that?
      • Sent him the healthcare.gov link and advised him if he wanted lower cost insurance to run it through the marketplace, and if he wants to run it through the business, it is typically more expensive. He asked if there was a way to write off his costs for personal healthcare in the business and I referred him to an accountant to answer that question.
    • Consulting jobs outside the US
      • Wants to work with Poland and maybe other cities in Europe and Canada
      • Wants to understand more fully about when he needs a visa, if a business visa would be appropriate, and how to keep track of out of country dealings to navigate any financial ramifications as well.
      • I will reach out

No-call, no-show.

Nancy and Denys discussed the outcome of a recent pitch, focusing on areas for improvement and future strategies. They addressed marketing approaches, including the potential hiring of a marketing firm and the creation of a demo video, while also considering legal aspects such as trademarking and patent filing. The conversation covered various aspects of their startup's development, including community engagement, product progress, and balancing social presence with project dedication.

Next steps

Denys to cut to the chase immediately in future pitches, clearly differentiating the product from Faceboo

Background:

  • Family business 99th year / 100th season
  • Started as a restaurant, Indian museum, gift shop
  • Part of the Mackinaw Crossings
  • Teysens pay 5% of the common ground expenses

Update since last meeting:

  • Business evaluation was done 3 years ago
  • Met with Don Molosky (appt in 2 weeks)
  • One person owns most of the hotels

Best way to sell business:

  • Don’t want to sell the name
  • Sell the business as is
  • Sell the real estate

What to do next:

  • Value the business
  • Appraisal on the real estate
  • Prep for the meeting with Don Molosky (reduce hourly fees)

Range of Value (Business only):

  • Revenue X .75 (industry standard) = $600K
  • EBITDA: $520-600K
  • Sellers discretionary earnings: $540K
  • DCF: $800 - $1 m
Co-Consult with Jim McLain. Filled in for Brooks. Developed "back-of-envelope" business valuations and discussed path to selling.

Following the success of the Michigan Developer's Conference, Paul is seeking funding through the MEDC for scaling the operation with participation from many of the educational institutions across MI. It is not clear what budget this would come out of, but he referred to a discussion in the fall with Bill Mayer of Spark, to whom he is delivering the proposal. I advised splitting the game development and event management efforts into two separate entities to avoid possible concerns over commingling funds. The game development side has been delayed due to a family emergency for his co founder, but they are readying for a May launch of their first project. He has set up a discord for coordinating across the state colleges, and there will be regular virtual meetings with a laudatory initial effort to advocate for college credits for cross discipline and/or cross institutional game projects. If funds can be raised to incentivize and combine the highest talent across the state, it wi

Joseph has been trying to find a loan for his business expansion. I had agreed to help since he is unhappy with his current bank.


I had an extended conversation with Jeff Cameron at Huntington and had reached out to Nicole at CNB, Erik at Huron State Bank and Wendy Lee at Wolverine CU to see about their interest.


After my research, I summarized my research and sent the information to Joseph. I will be ready to assist after he make a decision.


email to Joseph:


Joseph, I had hoped to get information about lenders to you yesterday but needed one more conversation today.


There are three options for you to consider.

1. Huntington Bank

2. Citizens National Bank

3. Awakon Credit Union


Here is what I know about each option. Please review my comments and then decide how you want to proceed.


1. I had an extended discussion with my contact at Huntington. He
submitted market research request for how many boat owners there are in Michigan, how many marinas
I briefly spoke with our client. He is out on storm work in PA. He said he would call back.

Closing case. will reopen if client reaches back out.


Jordan Craven

PROBLEM

Gaining Momentum, yet trajectory not measurable


DISCUSSION

  1. Define the total costs and expenses
  2. Define the revenue sources
    1. Ticket Sales
    2. Sponsors
    3. COllaborations
  3. Genesee Health Systems' marketing campaign has been strong.
    1. she needs to capitalize on this to leverage her other marketing methods
  4. Marketing Strategy
    1. Nephew is a graphic designer - lacks strategic direction
    2. Strategic plan
    3. Outsource wisely
    4. Leverage your partners as you biggest promoters
      1. create a blueprint /template for your own marketing promotional package
      2. copy and past with partners face, name, logo, etc.... then give THEM a script /best practices on how to market it...

NEXT STEPS

  1. Define the marketing campaign
  2. Clearly define the driving priority/desired outcome for each partner - nurture it in purpose.
Run the weekly management meeting.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Summary of meeting with client today:


ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM


The client is entering their second year of business with positive momentum but still faces significant challenges to achieve growth and long-term sustainability. Their primary goal is to scale operations to detail 7 to 8 vehicles daily. A critical financial concern is an upcoming balloon payment on their property due in February 2026, which requires finding refinancing solutions. Additionally, the client is evaluating whether to restructure the business into two LLCs — one for the property (landlord) and another for the detailing services — or continue under a single LLC with a DBA. The client has also faced challenges with accounting services and is seeking reliable financial guidance moving for

May News From Your SBDC Consultant // Michelle Judd

Ordered market research and sent follow up email.

 

From: Marie Elliott

Sent: Wednesday, April 30, 2025 4:01 PM

To: aaron@backwoodsboystoys.com

Subject: Follow up


Hi Aaron,


It was nice talking with you and learning more about your existing Sporting Goods Store, your new land and opportunities at this new location. As promised, attached you will find the following information: <

Reviewed pitch deck and pitch notes prior to our meeting. Cathy practiced her pitch and was at 4 minutes 30 seconds.

She read from her notes, which allowed her to stay under the five minutes. I recommended she practice her pitch multiple times, so she feels comfortable with what she wants to say on each slide. We discussed back up plan if she gets nervous and needs to read from her notes page. I liked how she placed the slide number next to her content, if she loses her place.


She will make some changes to slides 4 and 5. I recommended she make sure her numbers match what they submitted in her business plan and projections. We spent some time reviewing the business plan and projections that were submitted so they can include some bullet points from the plan in her presentation. Pulled Vertical IQ to pull some facts from industry trends regarding the 10% increase in sales.


Plan: Phone meeting scheduled for tomorrow at 10am to practice the pitch

May News From Your SBDC Consultant // Michelle Judd

I met with Lisa Hill and Drusilla Fanning from Huntington Bank today to discuss a loan program for Lisa's purchase of Olsen's Party Planning. Drusilla presented. Lisa now has three loan programs to choose from. While we are waiting for the sellers to return from vacation, I'd like her to do the following:

  1. Read and understand the "Key Topics for Transaction" from John Carter. Questions, please let me know and I will forward to him.
  2. Read and understand the "Internal Business Valuation" . Questions, please let me know and I will forward to Dan Brophy. BTW "Supplier Concentrations": the answer would most likely be “NO” for a party equipment rental business.
  3. Conduct a business search on Google and Dun & Bradstreet,

May News From Your SBDC Consultant // Michelle Judd
